reasons: Angela supports Silvanna's committed engage with shield, sustain, and movement support.
This helps Silvanna stay alive while she traps and damages a target inside Imperial Justice.

proof.summary: Silvanna commits into the enemy with Imperial Justice, and Angela adds protection during that risky lock-target window.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches as Silvanna enters with ultimate.
Silvanna locks a valuable target instead of a tank.
failureCases: Angela attaches too late after Silvanna is already controlled.

reasons: Pharsa can punish Silvanna's restrained target from long range.
Silvanna's target lock gives Pharsa a clearer window to land ranged burst.

proof.summary: Silvanna restrains a target, while Pharsa follows from range with burst damage during the trapped window.
worksBestWhen: Silvanna locks a priority target that Pharsa can reach with spell range.
Pharsa holds her burst until Silvanna confirms the lock.
failureCases: Pharsa is zoned away before the follow-up.
The enemy interrupts Pharsa's casting window.

reasons: Lunox can follow Silvanna's trapped target with fast magic burst.
This helps finish the target before the Imperial Justice window ends.

proof.summary: Silvanna isolates the target, while Lunox adds burst damage to secure the pickoff.
worksBestWhen: Lunox is close enough to follow immediately.
Silvanna restrains a squishy or high-value target.
failureCases: The target survives the first burst and the enemy team counter-engages.

reasons: Vexana can follow Silvanna's restraint with delayed AoE burst and extra control pressure.
Silvanna's lock makes it easier for Vexana's delayed damage to connect.

proof.summary: Silvanna restrains a target in a fixed area, while Vexana follows with delayed AoE damage and Eternal Guard pressure.
worksBestWhen: Vexana casts after Silvanna confirms the target lock.
The trapped target is close to other enemies, increasing AoE value.
failureCases: Vexana casts too early and the enemy moves before Silvanna lands.
Silvanna locks a target outside Vexana's follow-up range.
The enemy disengages before Eternal Guard adds value.

reasons: Kadita can follow Silvanna's target lock with knock-up and burst damage.
This creates a strong pickoff sequence when both heroes commit to the same trapped target.

proof.summary: Silvanna holds the target in place, while Kadita adds delayed CC and burst during the trapped window.
worksBestWhen: Kadita times Breath of the Ocean after Silvanna confirms Imperial Justice.
Silvanna locks a target that Kadita can immediately reach.
failureCases: Both heroes commit into the same target without enough damage follow-up.
The enemy saves displacement or suppression to punish the double commit.
